item.title: Council on Foreign Relations Hiring Research Associate, Women and Foreign Policy Program in Washington, DC
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Professionals interested in international affairs, gender equality, foreign policy research, and global development have an opportunity to join one of the United States’ leading foreign policy institutions. The Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) is hiring a Research Associate for the Women and Foreign Policy program, a role designed for an early-career professional who wants to […]

The post Council on Foreign Relations Hiring Research Associate, Women and Foreign Policy Program in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals interested in international affairs, gender equality, foreign policy research, and global development have an opportunity to join one of the United States’ leading foreign policy institutions.

The Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) is hiring a Research Associate for the Women and Foreign Policy program, a role designed for an early-career professional who wants to contribute to research, publications, policy discussions, and initiatives focused on advancing women’s participation and leadership globally.

The position is part of CFR’s David Rockefeller Studies Program, one of the country’s largest and most respected foreign policy research programmes.

The successful candidate will support the Senior Fellow for Women and Foreign Policy by conducting research, coordinating events, assisting with publications, managing administrative responsibilities, and helping communicate research findings to policymakers, experts, and the public.

The position is based in:

Washington, DC, United States

Salary range:

$55,000 – $58,000 per year

About the Council on Foreign Relations

The Council on Foreign Relations is an independent, nonpartisan organisation dedicated to improving understanding of international affairs and advancing informed discussions about US foreign policy.

Through research, publications, events, and public engagement, CFR brings together experts, policymakers, scholars, and leaders to examine major global challenges.

The organisation’s David Rockefeller Studies Program focuses on producing research and analysis that influence conversations about:

  • US foreign policy
  • International security
  • Global economic issues
  • International relations

About the Women and Foreign Policy Program

The Women and Foreign Policy program examines how improving the status of women and girls contributes to international goals such as:

  • Peace
  • Economic prosperity
  • Security
  • Political stability

The programme conducts research and provides analysis on issues related to:

  • Gender equality
  • Women’s leadership
  • Political participation
  • International development
  • Economic empowerment
  • Foreign policy

Through research publications, expert discussions, briefings, and public engagement, the programme informs:

  • Policymakers
  • Opinion leaders
  • Researchers
  • The wider public

The Research Associate will play an important role in supporting this work.

Position Overview

The Research Associate will provide research, administrative, and programme support to the Senior Fellow for Women and Foreign Policy.

This is a full-time position requiring a combination of:

  • Policy research
  • Writing support
  • Event coordination
  • Administrative management
  • Communication assistance

The ideal candidate will be highly organised, detail-oriented, curious, and passionate about women’s international policy issues.

Key Responsibilities

Conducting Policy Research

The Research Associate will support research projects by:

  • Investigating assigned policy topics.
  • Conducting literature reviews.
  • Gathering and analysing information.
  • Tracking developments related to women’s issues and international economics.
  • Supporting articles, book chapters, web content, and other publications.

Supporting Publications and Written Materials

Responsibilities include:

  • Assisting with editing and proofreading.
  • Reviewing articles and research documents.
  • Supporting publication preparation.
  • Helping ensure written materials meet professional standards.

Providing Administrative Support

The role includes supporting the Senior Fellow through:

  • Managing correspondence.
  • Scheduling meetings.
  • Maintaining calendars.
  • Responding to information requests.
  • Providing organisational assistance.

Coordinating Events and Meetings

The Research Associate will assist with programme events by:

  • Preparing invitations.
  • Communicating with speakers and participants.
  • Coordinating travel arrangements.
  • Preparing background documents.
  • Supporting event logistics.

Events may include:

  • Roundtable discussions
  • Policy briefings
  • Expert conversations
  • Public programmes

Supporting Communications and Digital Outreach

The position includes assisting with:

  • Website content
  • Blog publishing
  • Social media outreach
  • Digital communication activities

The candidate may support platforms such as social media channels and online publications.

Supporting Budget and Grant Management

The Research Associate may assist with:

  • Tracking monthly financial statements.
  • Managing grant information.
  • Preparing grant reports.
  • Supporting reimbursement processes.

Required Qualifications

Education Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in:
    • International relations
    • Political science
    • Social sciences
    • Related fields

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • At least one relevant administrative or internship experience.

Preferred experience includes work related to:

  • Women’s issues
  • Gender equality
  • Political participation
  • Leadership
  • Development
  • International economics

Skills Required

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Research Skills

Including:

  • Policy research
  • Information analysis
  • Literature reviews
  • Data gathering

Communication Skills

Applicants should have:

  • Excellent writing skills.
  • Strong verbal communication abilities.
  • Ability to communicate complex topics clearly.

Organisation Skills

The role requires:

  • Strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to manage multiple responsibilities.
  • Effective time management.

Technical Skills

Applicants should have experience with:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft Excel

Experience with research databases such as ProQuest is preferred.

Digital Communication Skills

Candidates should understand:

  • Social media communication.
  • Digital outreach strategies.
  • Online publishing tools.

Workplace Location and Schedule

Position:

Research Associate, Women and Foreign Policy

Organisation:

Council on Foreign Relations

Location:

Washington, DC, United States

Employment type:

Full-time

The position requires regular office attendance.

Employees are required to work onsite at least three days per week, with Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays serving as institutional anchor days.

Beginning September 14, 2026, CFR employees will work onsite at least four days per week.

Salary and Benefits

Annual compensation:

$55,000 – $58,000

Salary depends on:

  • Professional experience
  • Qualifications

Benefits include:

  • Competitive nonprofit salary package
  • Health insurance programmes
  • Wellness programmes
  • Generous leave policies
  • On-site fitness centre

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Commitment

The Council on Foreign Relations is committed to building a foreign policy community that reflects diverse perspectives and experiences.

The organisation recognises that individuals from different backgrounds contribute to stronger policy discussions and deeper understanding of global issues.

CFR works to:

  • Expand diversity within its membership and staff.
  • Encourage broader participation in foreign policy discussions.
  • Develop opportunities for underrepresented groups in international affairs.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

1. Resume/CV

The resume should highlight:

  • Education
  • Research experience
  • Policy interests
  • Relevant internships or administrative experience

2. Cover Letter

The cover letter should explain:

  • Interest in the position
  • Relevant qualifications
  • Passion for women and foreign policy issues

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application through the official CFR job application portal.

Click here for more and to apply

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Research Associate role offers an excellent opportunity for an early-career professional to gain experience at the intersection of:

  • Gender equality
  • International affairs
  • Public policy
  • Research
  • Global development

By supporting the Women and Foreign Policy program, the successful candidate will contribute to research and conversations that shape understanding of how empowering women and girls strengthens societies worldwide.

Click here for more global job opportunities

 

The post Council on Foreign Relations Hiring Research Associate, Women and Foreign Policy Program in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027: Funding for Low-Income Students Pursuing Master’s Degrees in Scotland
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027 | Funding Support for Master’s Students from Low-Income Households in Scotland Pursuing a postgraduate degree can open doors to new career opportunities, advanced research, and professional growth. However, the financial cost of earning a master’s degree often prevents many talented students from continuing their education. To help address this challenge, the […]

The post Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027: Funding for Low-Income Students Pursuing Master’s Degrees in Scotland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027 | Funding Support for Master’s Students from Low-Income Households in Scotland

Pursuing a postgraduate degree can open doors to new career opportunities, advanced research, and professional growth. However, the financial cost of earning a master’s degree often prevents many talented students from continuing their education.

To help address this challenge, the Carnegie Education Fund has introduced the Carnegie Masters Grants for the 2026–2027 academic year. This initiative is designed to improve access to postgraduate education by providing financial assistance to eligible students living in lower-income households in Scotland.

The grant helps cover the remaining cost of tuition fees after the Scottish Government postgraduate tuition fee loan has been applied. In certain circumstances, the grant can also contribute towards additional study-related expenses, making postgraduate education more affordable and accessible.

If you have received an offer to study an eligible master’s programme at a Scottish university and meet the residency and financial requirements, this funding opportunity could significantly reduce the financial burden of your studies.

About the Carnegie Masters Grants

The Carnegie Masters Grants are funded by the Carnegie Education Fund to widen participation in postgraduate education across Scotland.

The programme specifically supports students who demonstrate financial need and aims to remove barriers that often prevent capable individuals from undertaking master’s-level education.

Unlike a loan, this grant provides funding that does not need to be repaid, helping students focus on their academic and professional goals rather than financial concerns.

What Does the Grant Cover?

Successful applicants may receive funding that includes:

  • A contribution towards Home-rate postgraduate tuition fees
  • Funding to cover the remaining tuition costs after the Student Awards Agency for Scotland (SAAS) postgraduate tuition fee loan has been applied
  • Additional financial assistance for eligible study-related expenses, which may include:
    • Travel costs
    • Childcare expenses
    • Essential equipment required for study

The information provided in the application will be used to assess eligibility for both tuition support and any additional financial assistance.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements.

You may be eligible if you:

  • Are ordinarily resident in Scotland
  • Have a meaningful connection to Scotland
  • Live in a lower-income household
  • Hold an offer to study an eligible postgraduate master’s programme at a Scottish university
  • Do not already possess a master’s qualification

Applications are accepted from students studying through different learning modes.

Eligible study options include:

  • Full-time master’s programmes
  • Part-time master’s programmes (minimum 0.5 FTE)
  • Distance learning master’s programmes

Eligible Master’s Programmes

The Carnegie Masters Grants support both:

  • Taught Master’s degrees
  • Research Master’s degrees

These programmes must be offered by eligible Scottish universities.

Courses That Are Not Eligible

The following programmes are not eligible for Carnegie Masters Grant funding:

  • Postgraduate Certificate (PGCert) programmes
  • Postgraduate Diploma (PGDip) programmes
  • Postgraduate Certificate in Education (PGCE)
  • Postgraduate Diploma in Education (PGDE)
  • Master of Business Administration (MBA) programmes
  • Master of Philosophy (MPhil)
  • Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes

Applicants should carefully confirm that their intended programme qualifies before submitting an application.

Application Process

Applying for the Carnegie Masters Grant involves two important stages.

Step 1: Apply for the SAAS Tuition Fee Loan

Applicants must first submit an application to the Student Awards Agency for Scotland (SAAS) for the postgraduate tuition fee loan.

The loan provides funding of up to £7,000 towards eligible tuition fees.

Step 2: Apply to the Carnegie Education Fund

After applying for the SAAS tuition fee loan, eligible applicants can then submit an application directly to the Carnegie Education Fund for additional financial support to cover the remaining tuition fee costs and, where applicable, extra study expenses.

Applications are submitted directly through the Carnegie Education Fund.

Application Timeline

Applications for the 2026–2027 academic year opened on:

1 May 2026

Application Deadline

31 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare all required documentation and complete both the SAAS tuition fee loan application and the Carnegie Masters Grant application well before the closing date.

Why Apply for the Carnegie Masters Grants?

The Carnegie Masters Grants offer several important benefits:

  • Reduces the financial burden of postgraduate education
  • Supports students from lower-income households
  • Helps cover tuition costs beyond the SAAS loan
  • May provide funding for travel, childcare, and study equipment
  • Supports full-time, part-time, and distance learning students
  • Encourages wider access to postgraduate education in Scotland
  • Enables students to pursue advanced qualifications without excessive financial pressure

Important Eligibility Summary

Applicants should ensure they:

  • Live in Scotland
  • Have a meaningful connection to Scotland
  • Meet the lower-income household requirements
  • Have received an offer for an eligible master’s programme
  • Have not previously completed a master’s degree
  • Apply for the SAAS postgraduate tuition fee loan before submitting their Carnegie Masters Grant application

Deadline

Application Deadline: 31 July 2026

Late applications may not be considered, so prospective students are encouraged to begin the application process as early as possible.

Learn More and Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and apply for the Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027 HERE.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Carnegie Masters Grants 2026–2027: Funding for Low-Income Students Pursuing Master’s Degrees in Scotland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027 in Amsterdam, Netherlands | Fully Funded 5-Month Fellowship for Writers from the Dutch Caribbean with €3,500 Monthly Stipend
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027, an exceptional five-month literary fellowship designed exclusively for established writers based in the Caribbean Parts of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. Jointly organized by the NIAS (Netherlands Institute for Advanced Study) and the Dutch Foundation for Literature (Nederlands Letterenfonds), this prestigious fellowship offers writers the […]

The post Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027 in Amsterdam, Netherlands | Fully Funded 5-Month Fellowship for Writers from the Dutch Caribbean with €3,500 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027, an exceptional five-month literary fellowship designed exclusively for established writers based in the Caribbean Parts of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. Jointly organized by the NIAS (Netherlands Institute for Advanced Study) and the Dutch Foundation for Literature (Nederlands Letterenfonds), this prestigious fellowship offers writers the opportunity to dedicate themselves full-time to an individual literary research or writing project while becoming part of an international interdisciplinary academic community in Amsterdam.

The fellowship provides generous financial support, including a monthly stipend of €3,500, travel reimbursement, subsidized accommodation, office space, and access to world-class academic resources. Fellows will spend five months (February–July 2027) working alongside scholars, researchers, and professionals from numerous disciplines, creating an environment that encourages intellectual exchange, collaboration, and creative inspiration.

The application period runs from 6 July 2026 until 25 August 2026 (12:00 noon Central European Time).

About the Fellowship

The Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship is a continuation of the long-running Writer-in-Residence programme established through the partnership between the Dutch Foundation for Literature and NIAS. Since 1999, this collaboration has welcomed renowned writers from around the world to Amsterdam, allowing them to develop ambitious literary projects while engaging with an international scholarly community.

For the 2026–2027 edition, the fellowship specifically focuses on writers based in the Caribbean Parts of the Kingdom, helping amplify Caribbean literary voices and encouraging dialogue between literature, research, culture, and society.

The fellowship provides an environment where literary creativity and academic research intersect, allowing writers to gain fresh perspectives, deepen their work, and contribute to discussions that extend beyond literature.

Fellowship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive fellowship package that includes:

  • A five-month residency at NIAS in Amsterdam.
  • A personal stipend of €3,500 per month.
  • Reimbursement for an international return flight ticket.
  • Subsidized accommodation in Amsterdam (where applicable).
  • A private office at NIAS.
  • Full access to NIAS library and research facilities.
  • Participation in academic seminars and interdisciplinary discussions.
  • Opportunities to engage with leading international scholars.
  • Access to outreach programmes and community activities.
  • A supportive environment designed specifically for sustained literary work.

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship will run for:

  • February 2027 – July 2027
  • Total duration: Five months

During this period, fellows are expected to dedicate themselves fully to their proposed literary project.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowship is open to literary writers who satisfy all of the following criteria:

Applicants must be based in one of the Caribbean Parts of the Kingdom:

  • Aruba
  • Bonaire
  • Curaçao
  • Saba
  • Sint Eustatius
  • Sint Maarten

Applicants must write in one or more of the following languages:

  • Papiamentu
  • Papiamento
  • English from the Islands
  • Dutch

Applicants should also:

  • Be established literary fiction or non-fiction writers.
  • Have a strong literary publication record.
  • Demonstrate sustained literary excellence.
  • Be at the mid-career or senior stage of their writing career.
  • Be comfortable working in an international academic environment.
  • Have a good command of English, the working language at NIAS.
  • Be willing to actively participate in seminars, workshops, and intellectual exchanges.
  • Submit a literary project that is already beyond the idea stage and sufficiently developed for productive work during the residency.

What Kind of Projects Are Eligible?

Applicants are expected to propose a literary project that will clearly benefit from the unique research environment at NIAS.

Projects should:

  • Already be substantially developed.
  • Demonstrate clear objectives.
  • Show strong literary quality.
  • Be feasible within the five-month fellowship period.
  • Benefit from interaction with scholars from multiple disciplines.

Both literary fiction and literary non-fiction projects are welcome.

Expectations During the Fellowship

The residency is considered a full-time commitment.

Selected fellows are expected to:

  • Be present at NIAS for four working days each week.
  • Participate in weekly seminars.
  • Join interdisciplinary discussions.
  • Engage with fellow researchers and scholars.
  • Contribute to the intellectual life of the institute.
  • Work consistently on their literary project throughout the fellowship period.

Selection Criteria

Applications first undergo an eligibility review by the Dutch Foundation for Literature.

Eligible applications are then evaluated by an independent ranking committee appointed by the Foundation.

Applications are assessed primarily on:

  • Literary quality of previous work.
  • Strength and originality of the proposed project.
  • Feasibility of completing meaningful work during the fellowship.
  • Suitability of the applicant for the NIAS interdisciplinary environment.

Following the committee’s evaluation:

  1. The Dutch Foundation for Literature prepares a ranked recommendation.
  2. NIAS reviews the recommendations.
  3. The final selection is made in consultation with the NIAS Academic Advisory Board.

Only one fellow will be selected for the 2026–2027 programme.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

Unlike many writing residencies, the Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship places writers within one of Europe’s leading interdisciplinary research institutes.

Participants gain:

  • Time dedicated exclusively to writing.
  • Financial stability throughout the residency.
  • Access to leading academics and researchers.
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration.
  • International networking opportunities.
  • A vibrant intellectual community in Amsterdam.
  • Resources that can significantly enrich literary research and creative work.

This makes the programme particularly valuable for experienced writers seeking an environment that combines literary creativity with scholarly engagement.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: 6 July 2026
  • Application Deadline: 25 August 2026 (12:00 noon CET)
  • Fellowship Period: February – July 2027

How to Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and apply for the Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027 HERE.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Nederlands Letterenfonds Fellowship 2027 in Amsterdam, Netherlands | Fully Funded 5-Month Fellowship for Writers from the Dutch Caribbean with €3,500 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Helsinki Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism 2026 in Finland: Fully Funded Four-Year PhD Research Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The University of Helsinki has opened applications for a Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism, offering an exceptional four-year funded doctoral research position for aspiring researchers interested in humanitarian systems, refugee studies, development, migration, anthropology, and global social change. Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences, this prestigious doctoral position forms part of the […]

The post University of Helsinki Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism 2026 in Finland: Fully Funded Four-Year PhD Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Helsinki has opened applications for a Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism, offering an exceptional four-year funded doctoral research position for aspiring researchers interested in humanitarian systems, refugee studies, development, migration, anthropology, and global social change.

Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences, this prestigious doctoral position forms part of the Research Council of Finland-funded project, “New Humanitarian Infrastructures at the End of Aid,” led by Dr. Stefan Millar. The successful applicant will begin the position on 20 November 2026 and undertake cutting-edge research exploring how humanitarian systems are evolving in response to major global shifts in international aid.

The project provides doctoral researchers with the opportunity to conduct original ethnographic research, collaborate with international partners, contribute to academic publications, and complete a PhD at one of Europe’s leading research universities while enjoying the excellent academic and social environment that Finland offers.

Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 (23:59 Helsinki Time).

About the University of Helsinki

The University of Helsinki is one of Europe’s leading multidisciplinary research universities, recognized globally for excellence in research, innovation, and education.

With over:

  • 30,000 students
  • 8,000 staff members
  • Extensive international collaborations
  • Strong commitment to diversity, inclusion, and scientific excellence

the university provides an outstanding environment for doctoral education and research.

Its mission is built around four core values:

  • Truth
  • Bildung (education and intellectual growth)
  • Freedom
  • Inclusivity

Through interdisciplinary collaboration, the University of Helsinki seeks to generate solutions to some of the world’s most pressing social, environmental, and humanitarian challenges.

About the Research Project

The doctoral researcher will join the project:

New Humanitarian Infrastructures at the End of Aid

The project investigates one of the most important transformations occurring in global humanitarian assistance today.

Following major reductions in international aid—including the withdrawal of USAID support in 2025—the global humanitarian landscape is undergoing profound structural changes.

Rather than viewing humanitarian systems solely as institutional aid organizations, the project conceptualizes humanitarian infrastructures as dynamic assemblages involving:

  • Social relationships
  • Digital technologies
  • Material systems
  • Financial networks
  • Government institutions
  • Refugee-led initiatives
  • Private-sector actors
  • Diaspora communities

The project seeks to understand how these interconnected systems are evolving as traditional international aid declines.

Research Focus

Although the primary empirical focus is Kenya, applicants may propose research in another relevant humanitarian context if appropriately justified.

The project combines theoretical innovation with practical field research and aims to contribute to wider international debates concerning:

  • The future of humanitarianism
  • Refugee-led governance
  • Alternative aid systems
  • Humanitarian innovation
  • Global inequality
  • Humanitarian infrastructures
  • Social transformation

Core Research Themes

Applicants should align their proposed doctoral research with one or more of the project’s major themes.

1. State Infrastructures

Research may examine how governments increasingly shape humanitarian responses by:

  • Developing state-led humanitarian programmes
  • Expanding social protection systems
  • Replacing traditional aid agencies
  • Restructuring refugee governance

2. Refugee-Led Infrastructures

This theme investigates how refugees themselves create alternative humanitarian systems through:

  • Community-based organizations
  • Grassroots leadership
  • Political movements
  • Local humanitarian initiatives
  • Mutual support networks

Applicants interested in participatory research or community-led development are especially encouraged.

3. Transnational Remittance Infrastructures

Researchers may explore how diaspora communities support humanitarian needs through:

  • Digital finance
  • Mobile money
  • Remittance systems
  • Cross-border family support
  • Alternative aid distribution models

4. Corporate Humanitarian Infrastructures

This research theme focuses on the growing role of:

  • Private companies
  • Financial technology
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Digital humanitarian platforms
  • Predictive technologies

in shaping humanitarian governance and aid distribution.

Role and Responsibilities

The selected doctoral researcher will undertake independent research while contributing actively to the wider research project.

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting original ethnographic fieldwork
  • Designing and implementing a doctoral research project
  • Producing a doctoral dissertation (monograph or article-based)
  • Publishing research outputs
  • Collaborating with project partners
  • Organizing academic events
  • Supporting project communications
  • Participating in interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Undertaking teaching duties (up to 5% of annual working hours)

The project also encourages innovative methodologies, including collaborative and participatory research approaches.

Research Location

Primary fieldwork is expected to take place in:

  • Kenya

Alternative humanitarian contexts may also be proposed where academically justified.

During employment, the successful applicant is expected to reside in Finland, where the University of Helsinki will provide relocation support through its International Services team.

Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s degree in Social and Cultural Anthropology

or another closely related discipline, including:

  • Human Geography
  • Development Studies
  • Migration Studies
  • Refugee Studies
  • Sociology
  • Public Health
  • Political Science
  • Peace and Conflict Research

The Master’s degree should normally be completed before employment begins.

Applicants must also demonstrate:

  • Strong academic motivation
  • Capacity for doctoral research
  • Independent analytical thinking
  • Excellent academic writing ability
  • Commitment to completing a PhD

Additional Qualifications

The selection committee welcomes applicants with:

  • Refugee backgrounds
  • East African backgrounds
  • Experience working in humanitarian settings
  • Experience living in humanitarian contexts
  • Previous research experience in Kenya or similar contexts

Language skills in any of the following are considered an advantage:

  • Swahili
  • Somali
  • Arabic
  • Other languages relevant to humanitarian research

Doctoral Study Requirement

The successful candidate must have secured admission to doctoral studies at the University of Helsinki before employment begins.

If doctoral study rights have not yet been obtained, they must be secured during the six-month probationary period.

Salary and Benefits

The University of Helsinki offers an attractive employment package.

Salary

Annual gross salary ranges approximately between:

  • €32,000
  • €42,000

depending on qualifications and previous experience.

Employment Benefits

Employees receive:

  • Finnish pension benefits
  • Occupational healthcare
  • Four-year funded employment
  • Academic mentoring
  • International research environment
  • Relocation support
  • Professional development opportunities

Why Study and Work in Finland?

Finland consistently ranks among the world’s best countries for:

  • Education
  • Quality of life
  • Work-life balance
  • Family wellbeing
  • Research excellence
  • Innovation

The Helsinki region offers:

  • World-class research facilities
  • Affordable housing
  • Excellent public transportation
  • Safe communities
  • High-quality healthcare
  • Free schooling (including English-language options)

Finland has also been ranked among the world’s best destinations for expatriate families.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following PDF documents:

1. Motivation Letter

Maximum:

  • One page

The letter should explain:

  • Interest in the project
  • Relevant background
  • Suitability for the position

2. Research Plan

Maximum:

  • Two pages (including bibliography)

The proposal should clearly explain:

  • Research questions
  • Theoretical framework
  • Methodology
  • Fieldwork plans
  • Alignment with project themes

3. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applicants should prepare a detailed academic CV highlighting:

  • Education
  • Research experience
  • Publications
  • Fieldwork
  • Relevant professional experience
  • Language skills

APPLY HERE

Recruitment Process

Following application review:

  • Shortlisted candidates will be invited to online interviews.

Interviews are expected during:

  • First week of August 2026
  • Second week of August 2026

Diversity and Inclusion

The University of Helsinki strongly promotes an equitable and inclusive academic community.

Applications are encouraged from candidates of diverse backgrounds, particularly those traditionally underrepresented in higher education and research.

The university places significant value on equality, accessibility, and inclusive excellence.

Important Dates

Activity Date
Application Deadline 24 July 2026 (23:59 Helsinki Time)
Interviews First and Second Weeks of August 2026
Employment Begins 20 November 2026
Employment Duration Four Years

Why You Should Apply

This doctoral position offers a rare opportunity to contribute to internationally significant research on the future of humanitarian systems during a period of unprecedented global change. Researchers will investigate real-world humanitarian transformations while working alongside leading scholars in anthropology, migration, development, and humanitarian studies.

Beyond financial support, the position provides access to world-class academic supervision, international collaboration, interdisciplinary research, ethnographic fieldwork, and a vibrant scholarly community at one of Europe’s top universities. Whether your interests lie in refugee-led innovation, humanitarian governance, digital aid systems, or state transformation, this programme offers an outstanding platform to build an impactful research career while earning a fully funded PhD.

Visit HERE to learn more about the University of Helsinki Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism 2026 in Finland.

Click HERE to access the FAQs

Conclusion

The University of Helsinki Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers who are passionate about understanding the changing nature of humanitarian action, refugee systems, and global development. Through a fully funded four-year doctoral position, successful candidates will engage in meaningful ethnographic research, contribute to internationally recognized scholarship, collaborate with leading experts, and help shape new knowledge on humanitarian infrastructures in a rapidly changing world. If you have a strong academic background, an interest in humanitarian studies, and a desire to produce research with global relevance, this doctoral position offers an ideal pathway to advance your academic career while making a lasting contribution to society.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post University of Helsinki Doctoral Researcher in the Anthropology of Humanitarianism 2026 in Finland: Fully Funded Four-Year PhD Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 in London (Paid): Apply for a Creative Innovation Internship in the Creative Play Lab
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 is now open for applications from students and recent graduates who are passionate about creativity, innovation, concept development, and the future of play. Based at the LEGO® Group’s London Hub, this paid internship offers an exciting opportunity to work with the Creative Play Lab, where interns will collaborate […]

The post LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 in London (Paid): Apply for a Creative Innovation Internship in the Creative Play Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 is now open for applications from students and recent graduates who are passionate about creativity, innovation, concept development, and the future of play. Based at the LEGO® Group’s London Hub, this paid internship offers an exciting opportunity to work with the Creative Play Lab, where interns will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to invent new play experiences for children around the world.

Starting in September 2026, the internship provides hands-on experience in early-stage ideation, creative problem-solving, concept development, and prototyping. Interns will work alongside designers, researchers, engineers, innovators, and technologists while developing their own creative skills and contributing to the next generation of LEGO® play experiences.

If you are an aspiring creative thinker who enjoys exploring “what if?” ideas and turning imagination into reality, this internship could be the perfect opportunity to launch your career.

About the LEGO® Group Creative Play Lab

The Creative Play Lab is LEGO® Group’s forward-looking innovation hub dedicated to imagining the future of play.

The team focuses on developing groundbreaking play experiences by combining:

  • Creative experimentation
  • Advanced technologies
  • Research into children’s play behaviours
  • Design thinking
  • Rapid prototyping
  • Cross-disciplinary collaboration

Rather than simply improving existing products, the Creative Play Lab explores entirely new ways children can play, learn, and engage with creative experiences.

Its mission is to ensure LEGO® continues to inspire imagination for generations to come.

About the Play Ideation Internship

As a Play Ideation Intern, you will become part of the Play Discovery department, where innovation begins.

Interns contribute fresh perspectives, cultural insights, creative energy, and original thinking while learning directly from experienced professionals across multiple disciplines.

Throughout the internship, participants will work on:

  • Personal curiosity projects
  • Team innovation missions
  • Creative capability-building initiatives
  • Concept exploration
  • Early-stage prototype development

Each day presents new challenges designed to stretch creativity and encourage experimentation.

Meet the Play Ideation Team

The Play Ideation Team specializes in transforming early concepts into exciting possibilities.

Their work includes:

  • Exploring future play opportunities
  • Developing early-stage concepts
  • Helping teams visualize ideas
  • Testing new creative directions
  • Building playful experiences
  • Creating environments that encourage innovation and collaboration

The team’s philosophy revolves around asking “What if?” and transforming those questions into the next generation of LEGO® experiences.

Internship Details

  • Position: Play Ideation Intern
  • Location: London, United Kingdom
  • Department: Creative Play Lab – Play Discovery
  • Management Level: Support
  • Category: Administration & Facilities
  • Job ID: 0000036028

Internship Duration

  • Start Date: 7 September 2026
  • Duration: Four months (until the end of December 2026)
  • Extension: Possible, depending on business needs
  • Working Arrangement: Full-time or Part-time

Responsibilities

Interns will actively contribute to creative projects and innovation initiatives.

Typical responsibilities include:

  • Supporting creative ideation workshops.
  • Facilitating brainstorming sessions.
  • Transforming early ideas into visual concepts.
  • Exploring independent creative directions.
  • Developing innovative play concepts.
  • Creating concept visualizations.
  • Building prototypes.
  • Encouraging teams to think beyond conventional ideas.
  • Collaborating with multidisciplinary teams.

Every assignment is designed to strengthen creativity, collaboration, and innovation skills.

What LEGO® Is Looking For

Successful applicants will demonstrate three essential qualities.

Curiosity

Applicants should:

  • Ask thoughtful questions.
  • Explore new possibilities.
  • Investigate challenges deeply.
  • Maintain an experimental mindset.
  • Constantly wonder “What if?”

Bravery

Interns should feel comfortable:

  • Sharing original ideas.
  • Receiving constructive feedback.
  • Working in uncertain environments.
  • Collaborating with diverse teams.
  • Taking creative risks.

Focus

Candidates should be able to:

  • Adapt to changing priorities.
  • Balance multiple projects.
  • Work independently.
  • Deliver creative solutions under evolving requirements.

Desired Skills

Although not mandatory, experience in any of the following areas will strengthen an application:

  • Sketching
  • Graphic design
  • Animation
  • Concept art
  • LEGO® building
  • Videography
  • Video editing
  • Music
  • Fashion
  • Dance
  • Illustration
  • Facilitation
  • Cultural research
  • Creative hobbies

LEGO® values diverse creative experiences and encourages applicants to showcase their unique talents.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit four separate PDF documents.

1. A4 Landscape Poster

Design an original A4 landscape poster explaining why you are an excellent fit for the internship.

Applicants may use:

  • Photography
  • Typography
  • Illustration
  • Colour
  • Graphic design
  • Visual storytelling

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Provide an up-to-date CV highlighting education, skills, and relevant experience.

3. Portfolio

Submit a comprehensive portfolio demonstrating:

  • Personal projects
  • Academic work
  • Creative experiments
  • Hobbies
  • Design thinking
  • Passion projects

4. Cover Letter

Applicants must submit a bespoke cover letter of no more than 200 words.

LEGO® encourages originality and authenticity rather than generic applications.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

Saturday, 18 July 2026

Late applications will not be considered.

Because applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Internship Benefits

Successful interns will enjoy numerous professional and personal benefits.

These include:

Career Development

  • Work alongside world-class innovators.
  • Learn from multidisciplinary professionals.
  • Build an international professional network.
  • Gain real-world innovation experience.

Creative Environment

Interns will work inside the LEGO® Group’s London Hub, including the highly innovative “Red Brick” area dedicated to creative experimentation.

Collaboration with Children

Participants will have opportunities to co-create with children, whose creativity and imagination inspire LEGO® innovation.

Employee Benefits

Eligible interns may also receive:

  • Enhanced family care leave.
  • Life insurance.
  • Disability insurance.
  • Mental health and wellbeing resources.
  • Employee discounts on LEGO® products.
  • Performance bonus (where eligible).
  • Hybrid working arrangements, averaging three office days per week.

Diversity and Inclusion

The LEGO® Group is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected.

Applications are welcomed from individuals of all backgrounds regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Gender identity
  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • National origin
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • Age

The company also maintains a strong commitment to children’s rights and child safeguarding.

Successful candidates working closely with children may be required to complete child safeguarding background screening before employment.

Why You Should Apply

The Play Ideation Internship is much more than a traditional internship. It is an opportunity to work at one of the world’s most innovative toy companies while helping shape the future of creative play.

Whether your interests lie in design, storytelling, innovation, art, technology, or creative problem-solving, this internship offers an environment where imagination is encouraged and original ideas are celebrated.

If you dream of transforming creative ideas into experiences that inspire millions of children worldwide, this internship offers an ideal starting point.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application in English through the official LEGO® Careers portal.

Ensure you upload:

  1. An A4 landscape poster.
  2. Your CV.
  3. A portfolio.
  4. A tailored cover letter (maximum 200 words).

Apply Here

Applications must be completed before Saturday, 18 July 2026.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global paid internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 in London (Paid): Apply for a Creative Innovation Internship in the Creative Play Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026: Fully Funded Residency for Emerging U.S. Artists Exploring Mental Health and Well-Being
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026 is now accepting applications from emerging artists across the United States who are passionate about using creativity to advance conversations around youth mental health, well-being, healing, and social change. As Hopelab launches its inaugural Artist-in-Residence Program, it is inviting four emerging artists to spend three months developing new or existing […]

The post Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026: Fully Funded Residency for Emerging U.S. Artists Exploring Mental Health and Well-Being appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026 is now accepting applications from emerging artists across the United States who are passionate about using creativity to advance conversations around youth mental health, well-being, healing, and social change.

As Hopelab launches its inaugural Artist-in-Residence Program, it is inviting four emerging artists to spend three months developing new or existing creative projects that explore the intersection of art, liberatory mental health, youth well-being, and community transformation.

The residency combines financial support, mentorship, creative immersion, professional development, and public engagement, providing artists with the resources needed to deepen their artistic practice while contributing to broader cultural conversations about youth well-being.

If you are an emerging artist committed to creating meaningful work that inspires healing and systemic change, this residency offers an exceptional opportunity to develop your vision with support from leading artists, researchers, and innovators.

About the Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program

Hopelab believes that meaningful social change begins by changing the stories people tell about themselves and their communities.

Historically, artists have played a central role in every movement for justice by imagining new possibilities before society was ready to embrace them. Through storytelling, music, visual arts, literature, and other creative forms, artists help communities process grief, celebrate joy, and imagine healthier futures.

Recognizing this power, Hopelab created its Artist-in-Residence Program to engage young artists in shaping new narratives around youth mental health and well-being.

Rather than focusing solely on research or policy, the residency centers creativity as an essential tool for healing, liberation, and cultural transformation.

Participants will have the opportunity to create original work—or complete existing projects—that reflects the intersection of:

  • Art
  • Mental health
  • Youth well-being
  • Community healing
  • Social justice
  • Narrative change
  • Cultural transformation
  • Collective care
  • Future-building

Why Hopelab Created This Residency

Hopelab recognizes that dominant narratives surrounding youth mental health have often excluded the voices and lived experiences of young people themselves.

This residency aims to change that by empowering artists to tell stories that:

  • Challenge existing assumptions.
  • Expand public understanding of well-being.
  • Inspire hope and imagination.
  • Promote healing-centered approaches.
  • Encourage systemic transformation.
  • Center communities historically overlooked by traditional mental health systems.

By bringing artists into conversation with researchers, youth advocates, and technologists, Hopelab hopes to create new cultural narratives that support healthier futures for young people.

What Residents Will Receive

Selected artists will receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support throughout the residency.

1. Living Stipend

Residents will receive a $7,500 living stipend to support their basic living expenses during the three-month residency.

2. Materials Budget

Each artist will receive an additional $2,500 materials budget.

The budget is intentionally flexible and artist-directed, allowing residents to purchase the materials, equipment, or creative resources most relevant to their artistic practice.

3. Professional Mentorship

Artists will receive mentorship from:

  • A practicing professional artist.
  • The Hopelab team.
  • The Djerassi Resident Artists Program team.

This guidance is designed to strengthen both artistic development and professional growth.

4. Fully Funded Creative Retreat

Residents will participate in a one-week immersive retreat at the renowned Djerassi Resident Artists Program in Northern California’s Santa Cruz Mountains.

Hopelab will fully cover:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation
  • Meals

The retreat provides uninterrupted time for artistic exploration while building meaningful relationships with fellow artists.

5. Access to Experts

Participants will connect with Hopelab’s network of:

  • Researchers
  • Mental health experts
  • Youth advocates
  • Technologists
  • Creative leaders

These interdisciplinary conversations are intended to enrich participants’ creative work and expand their perspectives.

6. Public Showcase

Artists will present their completed work during a public showcase in San Francisco in November 2026.

Hopelab will cover:

  • Travel
  • Accommodation
  • Meals

for participation in the showcase.

7. Professional Support

Participants will also receive:

  • A professional letter of support
  • Documentation of their completed artistic work

These materials can strengthen future grant, residency, fellowship, and exhibition applications.

8. Artist Database

Every applicant—whether selected or not—will be included in Hopelab’s digital artist database, increasing visibility within Hopelab’s creative network.

Who Hopelab Is Looking For

Hopelab seeks a diverse cohort of emerging artists who:

  • Are between 18 and 29 years old.
  • Live anywhere in the United States.
  • Work across one or multiple artistic disciplines.
  • Explore themes related to mental health and well-being.
  • Come from communities historically underserved by mental health systems.
  • Maintain strong connections to their communities through organizing, culture, mutual aid, or collective care.
  • Demonstrate originality and a compelling artistic vision.
  • Are committed to participating in every aspect of the residency.

Importantly, applicants do not need a university degree or previous institutional recognition.

Selection emphasizes:

  • Lived experience
  • Community engagement
  • Artistic vision
  • Commitment to the residency
  • Potential for impact

Residency Timeline

The residency follows the schedule below:

  • June 22, 2026 – Applications open
  • July 17, 2026 – Application deadline
  • July 27–31, 2026 – Finalist interviews and selection announcement
  • August 10 – November 6, 2026 – Three-month residency with biweekly cohort meetings and guest lectures
  • October 5–11, 2026 – In-person residency at Djerassi Resident Artists Program
  • Around November 5, 2026 – Public showcase in San Francisco

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • A work sample
  • An artist statement
  • Community context information

These materials help the selection committee understand each applicant’s creative vision, lived experience, and relationship with their community.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Artistic vision
  • Creative potential
  • Lived experience
  • Community rootedness
  • Alignment with the residency mission
  • Commitment to full participation

Rather than focusing on formal credentials, Hopelab values authenticity, imagination, and a demonstrated commitment to creating meaningful work.

Why You Should Apply

The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program offers much more than financial support.

Participants will gain:

  • Funding to pursue ambitious creative projects.
  • Professional mentorship.
  • National visibility.
  • Access to interdisciplinary experts.
  • A supportive artist community.
  • Fully funded travel and residency experiences.
  • Opportunities to showcase work publicly.
  • Long-term professional connections.

For emerging artists interested in shaping conversations around youth mental health and social transformation, this residency represents a rare opportunity to combine artistic practice with meaningful cultural impact.

How to Apply

Interested artists should submit their application through Hopelab’s official Artist-in-Residence application portal before the deadline.

Applicants should ensure they prepare:

  1. Work sample.
  2. Artist statement.
  3. Community context information.

Applications must be submitted by July 17, 2026.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026 is a unique opportunity for emerging U.S.-based artists to receive generous financial support, mentorship, and national exposure while creating work that advances youth mental health and well-being.

By bringing together creativity, community, and research, Hopelab aims to empower a new generation of artists whose work can inspire healing, challenge dominant narratives, and imagine healthier futures for young people.

Artists who are passionate about storytelling, social change, and community well-being are strongly encouraged to apply before July 17, 2026.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026: Fully Funded Residency for Emerging U.S. Artists Exploring Mental Health and Well-Being appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations World Health Organization Internship Program 2026 | Paid Internships for Students & Recent Graduates Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Looking to launch your career in global health? The World Health Organization (WHO) Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of the world’s leading public health organizations. The WHO Internship Programme provides hands-on exposure to international health initiatives, allowing interns to work alongside […]

The post United Nations World Health Organization Internship Program 2026 | Paid Internships for Students & Recent Graduates Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Looking to launch your career in global health? The World Health Organization (WHO) Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of the world’s leading public health organizations.

The WHO Internship Programme provides hands-on exposure to international health initiatives, allowing interns to work alongside global experts, strengthen their professional skills, and contribute to improving health outcomes worldwide. With opportunities available throughout the year across multiple countries, this paid internship is ideal for aspiring professionals in public health, medicine, communications, engineering, law, data science, administration, and many other fields.

About the WHO Internship Programme

The WHO Internship Programme is designed to develop the next generation of global health professionals by providing meaningful learning experiences in technical and administrative areas. Interns work in a multicultural environment alongside experts from diverse backgrounds while supporting WHO’s mission to promote health, keep the world safe, and serve vulnerable populations.

The programme helps participants gain valuable insight into international health systems, policy development, emergency response, research, and organizational operations while building professional networks across the globe.

Why Apply for the WHO Internship?

The WHO Internship Programme offers participants the opportunity to:

  • Gain practical experience in a globally recognized organization.
  • Work alongside leading public health and international development professionals.
  • Contribute to projects addressing global health challenges.
  • Build professional networks with experts from around the world.
  • Develop technical, analytical, and leadership skills.
  • Experience a multicultural and inclusive working environment.

WHO Internship Benefits

Successful applicants may receive several benefits, including:

  • Paid internship with financial support.
  • Living allowance for eligible interns.
  • Medical and accident insurance coverage during the internship.
  • Lunch vouchers at selected duty stations.
  • Professional supervision and mentorship.
  • Practical experience working on international health programmes.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge public health initiatives and health systems.

Internship Duration

The WHO Internship Programme lasts:

  • Minimum: 6 weeks
  • Maximum: 24 weeks

The exact duration depends on the department and internship assignment.

Who Can Apply?

The WHO Internship Programme is open to:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Master’s students
  • PhD students
  • Recent graduates

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible, applicants must:

  • Be at least 20 years old at the time of application.
  • Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate, graduate, or postgraduate programme, or have recently graduated.
  • Be studying at an institution recognized in the World Higher Education Database (WHED).
  • Provide proof of their academic qualifications.
  • Be fluent in at least one of WHO’s official working languages.
  • Hold a valid passport from a WHO Member State.
  • Have no immediate family relationship with a WHO staff member.
  • Not have previously participated in the WHO Internship Programme.

Required Documents

Applicants should prepare the following documents:

  • Updated CV or résumé
  • Academic transcripts
  • Academic letter of reference
  • Passport details
  • Application acknowledgment
  • WHO Medical Certificate (required before the internship begins)
  • Any additional documents requested for the specific internship

How to Apply

To apply for the WHO Internship Programme:

  1. Visit the official WHO Careers portal.
  2. Search for available internship opportunities.
  3. Select the internship that matches your qualifications.
  4. Complete the online application form.
  5. Provide your personal and academic information.
  6. Upload the required documents.
  7. Complete the Declaration of Interest form (where applicable).
  8. Review your application carefully and submit it.
  9. You will receive an email confirming your application submission.

Application Deadline

WHO internship opportunities are advertised throughout the year, with each vacancy having its own application deadline. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

WHO Internship Opportunities Available This Week

1. Internship – Health Emergencies

  • Location: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Organization: HQ/EPM – Epidemic & Pandemic Management
  • Contract Type: Intern
  • Application Deadline: 31 July 2026

 

APPLY NOW

 

2. Internship – Communications and Advocacy

  • Location: Berlin, Germany
  • Organization: HQ/EIS – Health Emergency Intelligence & Surveillance
  • Contract Type: Intern
  • Application Deadline: 30 July 2026

 

APPLY NOW

 

3. AI Engineering Intern – Data Engineering, Full-Stack Development, Quality & Evaluation

  • Location: Istanbul, Türkiye
  • Organization: EU/BOS – Division of Business Operations
  • Contract Type: Intern
  • Application Deadline: 21 July 2026

 

APPLY NOW

 

4. Internship – Health Systems, Noncommunicable Diseases & Communications

  • Location: Port Louis, Mauritius
  • Organization: WHO Country Office Mauritius
  • Contract Type: Intern
  • Application Deadline: 21 July 2026

APPLY NOW

 

5. Legal Intern

  • Location: Cairo, Egypt
  • Organization: EM/BOS – Business Operations
  • Contract Type: Intern
  • Application Deadline: 20 July 2026

 

APPLY NOW

 

Why You Shouldn’t Miss This Opportunity

The WHO Internship Programme is one of the world’s most prestigious international internship opportunities for students and recent graduates. Whether your interests lie in public health, medicine, engineering, communications, artificial intelligence, law, policy, or administration, the programme offers invaluable international experience, professional development, and the opportunity to contribute to improving health outcomes worldwide.

With paid benefits, mentorship from global experts, and opportunities available in multiple countries, the WHO Internship Programme can be an excellent stepping stone toward a career in international organizations and global health.

Apply Now

Applications are submitted through the official WHO Careers Portal. Since new internship opportunities are posted regularly throughout the year, applicants are encouraged to check the portal frequently and apply before the listed deadlines.

 

APPLY NOW

OFFICIAL LINK

The post United Nations World Health Organization Internship Program 2026 | Paid Internships for Students & Recent Graduates Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OECD Research Fellowships Research Fellowships 2027 | Fully Funded International Research Fellowships for Agricultural, Forestry & Fisheries Scientists
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
CRP Applications for Fellowships Applications are now open for the OECD Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) Research Fellowships 2027, offering scientists the opportunity to undertake 6–26 weeks of funded international research in another participating country. The programme supports cutting-edge research that advances sustainable agriculture, forestry, fisheries, food systems, climate resilience and natural resource management while fostering […]

The post OECD Research Fellowships Research Fellowships 2027 | Fully Funded International Research Fellowships for Agricultural, Forestry & Fisheries Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
CRP Applications for Fellowships

Applications are now open for the OECD Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) Research Fellowships 2027, offering scientists the opportunity to undertake 6–26 weeks of funded international research in another participating country. The programme supports cutting-edge research that advances sustainable agriculture, forestry, fisheries, food systems, climate resilience and natural resource management while fostering global scientific collaboration.

Key Details

  • Host Organisation: OECD Co-operative Research Programme (CRP)
  • Programme: CRP Research Fellowships 2027
  • Funding: Travel grant including return economy airfare, €600–€650 weekly subsistence allowance, and a €165 terminal allowance
  • Duration: 6–26 weeks
  • Eligible Applicants: Researchers working in agriculture, forestry or fisheries with at least four years of postdoctoral experience (or equivalent scientific expertise in exceptional cases). The programme is not intended for PhD students.
  • Research Location: Another participating OECD CRP member country
  • Eligible Countries: Researchers based in participating CRP countries, including the United Kingdom, United States, Canada, Australia, Brazil, Japan, Germany, France, Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, Spain, Ireland, New Zealand and others.
  • Research Areas: Sustainable agriculture, food security, climate change, biodiversity, forestry, fisheries, aquaculture, soil health, livestock systems, ecosystem services, Indigenous knowledge, water quality and agricultural innovation.
  • Application Deadline: 10 September 2026 (Midnight, Paris Time)
  • Results Announced: January 2027
  • Fellowship Start: From March 2027
  • Application Fee: None

Application process for fellowships

The  Co-operative Research Programme’s call for applications for funding research fellowship grants in 2027 is now OPEN.

The deadline to apply is Thursday 10 September 2026, midnight, Paris time.

General information

> Applicants must be residents in one of the countries participating in our Programme.  Please consult the files for general information on the country’s research institutes and universities.

Please note that:

  • Türkiye is a member until 24 December 2026 and will not participate in the call for applications for events and fellowships to be funded in 2027.
  • Brazil is a member of the CRP as of 1 July 2026, so Brazil is eligible to the call for applications for fellowships to be funded in 2027.
  • Ukraine is on ACCESSION STAGE. Should you want to have speakers from this country, please contact TAD.PROG@oecd.org to be updated on its status.

> Please consult the Event Guidelines and Conditions, Selection Criteria and the CRP Action Areas in the documents below.

> Applications relevant to the work of the OECD Committee for Agriculture and other bodies are particularly welcome:

• Sustainable and resilient productivity growth and food security and nutrition
• Climate risks, reducing emissions from agriculture and food systems, carbon sequestration in agriculture, forestry and land use
• Halting and reversing forest loss and land degradation
• Reducing the negative environmental impacts of livestock production and practices harmful to animal health and welfare, investigating the positive contribution livestock can make to soil quality and management, biodiversity and livelihoods
• Biodiversity, enhancing ecosystem services
• Improving soil health and water and air quality, including through agro-ecological and other innovative, context specific approaches
• Innovations in the transfer and development of agricultural knowledge, including Indigenous and traditional knowledge
• Fisheries and aquaculture productivity, sustainability and resilience

> Previous research fellowships are available for review. Testimonials from past fellows and their hosts can also be reviewed on the CRP home page (scroll down to the dedicated section).

Eligibility Requirements

Beyond being a researcher in agriculture, forestry or fisheries, applicants should also note that they must:

  • Be employed at an institution located in a participating CRP country.
  • Undertake the fellowship in another participating CRP country.
  • Normally have at least four years of postdoctoral experience. The programme is not intended for PhD students, although exceptional applicants without a PhD but with equivalent expertise and a strong publication record may be considered.
  • Have employer approval before submitting the application.
  • Have continued employment (or ongoing scientific affiliation) after the fellowship ends.
  • Previous CRP fellows may only apply again five years after receiving their first fellowship.

What Does the Fellowship Cover?

The OECD CRP Fellowship provides a travel lump-sum allowance consisting of three components:

  • Return economy-class travel between the fellow’s home institution and host institution
  • Weekly subsistence allowance of €600–€650 per week (depending on the cost of living in the host country)
  • A €165 terminal allowance to help cover transport costs at the beginning and end of the fellowship

What is Not Covered?

Applicants should budget separately for:

  • Medical and travel insurance
  • Visa and passport costs
  • Laboratory or bench fees
  • Daily commuting costs (covered within the subsistence allowance)
  • Travel or expenses for accompanying family members
  • Taxes and bank charges where applicable

To apply for a research fellowship award

1. please consult the following documents:

  • Guidelines and Conditions for CRP Research Fellowship Awards
  • Selection Criteria for CRP Fellowship Awards
  • Action Areas of the Co-operative Research Programme
  • Sample fellowship applications: Example 1; Example 2
  • Sample CV
  • Sample invitation letter
  • PDF Fellowship Application form

2. complete the following document:

  • Online application form for CRP Research Fellowships 

National correspondents and country files

The Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) currently has 32 participating countries with Brazil joining on 1 July 2026. Below is a list of countries, the contact information for their national correspondents and when available a [Country file], document which gives general information on the country’s research institutes and universities.

  • Australia                  Dr. Andy SHEPPARD
  • Austria                     Ms. Sanda PASC-WANDL                     [Country file]
  • Belgium                  Mme Véronique DEWASMES              [Country file]
  • Brazil                        Dr. Maria José A. M. SAMPAIO            [Country file]
  • Canada                    Ms Melanie SINGARAYER
  • Chile                         Mr Jack BRADY                                        [Country file]
  • Colombia                Mr. Felipe CORREAL CHARRIS
  • Costa Rica               Mr. José Fabián JIMÉNEZ MORALES
  • Czechia                    Ms. Šárka ŠTEJNAROVÁ                        [Country file]
  • Denmark                 Mr. Bjarne THOMSEN
  • Estonia                     Dr. Katri LING                                          [Country file]
  • Finland                     Prof. Pirjo PELTONEN-SAINIO            [Country file]
  • France                      Dr. Joachim HUET
  • Germany                 Prof. Dr. Jens DAUBER
  • Hungary                  Prof. András SZÉKÁCS
  • Ireland                     Ms. Ellen DURKIN
  • Israel                        Dr. Michal LEVY                                        [Country file]
  • Italy                          Ms. Silvia BARALLA
  • Japan                        Dr. Ichiro NAKAYAMA
  • Korea                        Ms Da-Som KANG
  • Latvia                        Ms Diāna LĪVA                                         [Country file]
  • Lithuania                 Ms Vilma KRAUJALYTE                           [Country file]
  • Netherlands           Ms. Saske HOVING                                 [Country file]
  • New Zealand          Ms. Shania BROOKS                               [Country file]
  • Norway                    Dr. Maan Singh SIDHU                          [Country file]
  • Slovak Republic     Dr. Kristína GENDOVÁ RUZSÍKOVÁ     [Country file]
  • Spain                        Dr. Violeta CARRASCO
  • Sweden                    Dr. Sara GRÄSLUND                               [Country file]
  • Switzerland             Ms Veronica CAGGIA
  • Türkiye                     Dr. Fatih ÖZDEMİR
  • United Kingdom    Mr. Alen BIJU                                            [Country file]
  • United States          Ms. Marcella WITTING

How Applications Are Evaluated

Applications are assessed against several selection criteria. Strong proposals should clearly demonstrate:

  • Scientific excellence and innovation
  • Relevance to the OECD CRP Action Areas
  • A feasible and well-designed research plan
  • Long-term collaboration between the applicant and host institution
  • Strong scientific achievements and publication record
  • Multidisciplinary approaches
  • Plans for dissemination of research findings
  • Practical impact of the research
  • Clear policy relevance for agriculture, forestry or fisheries

New international collaborations are given preference over existing collaborations

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Completed online application form
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • List of publications from the past five years
  • Signed invitation letter from the host institution
  • Research proposal addressing all selection criteria

Applications must be submitted in English.

Fellowship Timeline

  • Applications close: 10 September 2026
  • Award decisions: Mid-December 2026
  • Successful applicants notified: By January 2027
  • Recommended fellowship start: From 1 March 2027
  • Latest fellowship departure: 15 December 2027

Responsibilities of Successful Fellows

Recipients of the fellowship will be expected to:

  • Confirm arrival at the host institution
  • Complete an evaluation questionnaire after the fellowship
  • Submit a summary report within two months of completing the research
  • Acknowledge OECD CRP funding in any publications arising from the fellowship

Failure to submit the required final report may result in repayment of 10% of the fellowship award.

Contact

For any question regarding a fellowship application, please contact the CRP Secretariat: TAD.Prog@oecd.org

Source: https://www.oecd.org/en/about/programmes/co-operative-research-programme/crp-applications-for-fellowships.html

The post OECD Research Fellowships Research Fellowships 2027 | Fully Funded International Research Fellowships for Agricultural, Forestry & Fisheries Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 2026: 8-Week Remote Accelerator for African Climate-Tech Startups Building Food & Agriculture Solutions
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now open for the ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1, an intensive 8-week fully remote venture support programme designed specifically for early-stage founders developing climate adaptation and resilience solutions across Africa’s food and agriculture value chains. The programme supports founders who have already built a working prototype and are ready to validate their ideas […]

The post ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 2026: 8-Week Remote Accelerator for African Climate-Tech Startups Building Food & Agriculture Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1, an intensive 8-week fully remote venture support programme designed specifically for early-stage founders developing climate adaptation and resilience solutions across Africa’s food and agriculture value chains.

The programme supports founders who have already built a working prototype and are ready to validate their ideas with real users. Rather than focusing on theoretical business planning, Ignite helps startups move from assumptions to evidence by testing products in real-world environments, gathering customer feedback, refining business models, and preparing ventures for future investment and growth.

Unlike many startup accelerators that focus primarily on scaling businesses, ASIF Ignite places climate resilience at the center of innovation. The programme supports solutions that help vulnerable communities, especially smallholder farmers, better prepare for, withstand, recover from, and adapt to the growing impacts of climate change.

Applications remain open until 17 August 2026.

About ASIF Ignite Africa

ASIF Ignite is a cohort-based venture development programme that brings together promising early-stage founders from across Africa to validate climate adaptation innovations.

The programme is designed for entrepreneurs who have already moved beyond the idea stage by building a tangible product, service, technology, application, platform, device, or operating model, but who have not yet tested it extensively with their intended users.

Throughout the eight-week programme, participants receive structured guidance to:

  • Validate customer demand
  • Conduct real-world testing
  • Learn Lean Startup methodologies
  • Explore practical AI applications
  • Build measurable evidence of impact
  • Improve investor readiness
  • Strengthen business models

The programme is delivered entirely online, making it accessible to founders across Africa.

Why This Programme Matters

Climate change continues to place enormous pressure on Africa’s food systems.

Smallholder farmers increasingly face:

  • Droughts
  • Unpredictable rainfall
  • Flooding
  • Soil degradation
  • Crop losses
  • Poor market access
  • Rising temperatures
  • Food spoilage
  • Supply chain disruptions

Across Sub-Saharan Africa, up to one-third of food produced is lost before reaching consumers due to inadequate storage, poor logistics, and climate-related disruptions.

ASIF Ignite seeks founders developing practical innovations that improve resilience throughout the agricultural value chain.

Programme Focus for Africa Cohort 1

The first African cohort specifically focuses on food and agriculture value chains.

This includes every stage involved in producing food, including:

  • Farm inputs
  • Production
  • Water management
  • Farm technologies
  • Storage
  • Transportation
  • Processing
  • Distribution
  • Market access

The programme supports solutions that enable farmers and agribusinesses to prepare for climate shocks while maintaining sustainable livelihoods.

Solution Areas Supported

The programme welcomes innovations across multiple areas.

1. Inputs and Agricultural Knowledge

Solutions that improve farmers’ access to critical resources and information, including:

  • Climate-resilient seeds
  • Improved planting materials
  • Farm machinery
  • Irrigation systems
  • Greenhouse technologies
  • Agricultural input financing
  • Input logistics
  • Supply chain innovations
  • Weather-index insurance
  • Microcredit
  • Climate forecasting
  • Early warning systems
  • Agronomic advisory services
  • Solar-powered farm energy
  • Energy-efficient farming technologies

2. Growing Crops More Sustainably
